notes.content: DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION. Effective Fall 2018, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep Effective Fall 2015, the B.S. in Computer Science, the B.S. in Computer Engineering, and the B.S. in Computer Science with a specialization in Bioinformatics and the B.S. in Artificial Intelligence are impacted for transfer students. Visit cse.ucsd.edu for full information. General advice: Transfer students are advised to complete the following courses for their major before enrolling at UC San Diego. Preparing well for the major helps students move efficiently toward graduation. The following is a list of all required lower-division courses for the major: - Calculus I-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20A) - Calculus II-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20B) - Calculus and Analytic Geometry (Math. 20C) - Differential Equations (Math. 20D) - Linear Algebra (Math. 18) - Mechanics (Physics 2A) - Electricity and Magnetism (Physics 2B) - Fluids, Waves, Thermodynamics, and Optics (Physics 2C) - Introduction to Programming and Computational Problem Solving (CSE 11) Basic Data Structures and Object-Oriented Design (CSE 12) *Recommended: We recommend students take CSE 30, as this class is a requirement for UC San Diego Computer Engineering majors. Course equivalency: For course equivalencies not listed below, visit the CSE Student Affairs Office, CSE Building (EBU3B, Room 1200) first floor, or email csestudent@ucsd.edu. For information not found here, please visit the CSE Undergraduate Program at: https://cse.ucsd.edu/undergraduate
notes.contentHtml:
DATED MATERIAL, SUBJECT TO CHANGE. PLEASE CONSULT CURRENT UCSD GENERAL CATALOG FOR ANY ADDITIONAL INFORMATION.


Effective Fall 2018, major preparation will be required for this major. For details, visit: http://admissions.ucsd.edu/MajorPrep


Effective Fall 2015, the B.S. in Computer Science, the B.S. in Computer Engineering, and the B.S. in Computer Science with a specialization in Bioinformatics and the B.S. in Artificial Intelligence are impacted for transfer students. Visit cse.ucsd.edu for full information.


General advice: Transfer students are advised to complete the following courses for their major before enrolling at UC San Diego. Preparing well for the major helps students move efficiently toward graduation. The following is a list of all required lower-division courses for the major:


  1. - Calculus I-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20A)
  2. - Calculus II-for Science and Engineering (Math. 20B)
  3. - Calculus and Analytic Geometry (Math. 20C)
  4. - Differential Equations (Math. 20D)
  5. - Linear Algebra (Math. 18)
  6. - Mechanics (Physics 2A)
  7. - Electricity and Magnetism (Physics 2B)
  8. - Fluids, Waves, Thermodynamics, and Optics (Physics 2C)
  9. - Introduction to Programming and Computational Problem Solving (CSE 11)
  10. Basic Data Structures and Object-Oriented Design (CSE 12)


*Recommended: We recommend students take CSE 30, as this class is a requirement for UC San Diego Computer Engineering majors.


Course equivalency: For course equivalencies not listed below, visit the CSE Student Affairs Office, CSE Building (EBU3B, Room 1200) first floor, or email csestudent@ucsd.edu.


For information not found here, please visit the CSE Undergraduate Program at: https://cse.ucsd.edu/undergraduate
